what_it_does: Renders a full-width overlay called 'The fabric' that displays Company channels as a live graph by mounting DNA's generic channel-view archetype via window.DNA helpers, opened by the channels:open window event and closed by Esc, scrim click, or close button.
observations: A React overlay component driven by channelStore state (channels, loading, error, open).
Opens on a custom window event channels:open and closes on Escape when open.
Mounts DNA's fabric graph by calling window.DNA.faceRecord.channelGraph, window.DNA.org.hubNetwork, and window.DNA.renderArchetype.
Caches the fetched 'channel-view' archetype in window.DNA._channelViewArchetype.
Normalizes channel.members from an object to an array of ids before passing rows to channelGraph.
Degrades to plain text messages when DNA is unavailable, the archetype is missing, or rendering throws.
Uses aria attributes (role='dialog', aria-modal, aria-label, aria-hidden) on the overlay.
standouts: Heavy runtime dependency on a global window.DNA design substrate with no local fallback graph implementation.
Explicit data-shape adapter that converts members object→ids to match DNA's expected array input.
Generic renderArchetype pattern where the visual device (hubNetwork SVG string) is passed as an option.
conventions: Follows the overlay pattern: scrim, panel, header, close button, body messages.
Uses React useEffect for event subscription and graph mounting with cancellation/cleanup flags.
TypeScript type DNAGlobal documents the expected global DNA contract.
concerns: Relies entirely on window.DNA being present and correctly shaped; missing helpers produce a static 'isn’t ready' message rather than a rendered view.
Fetches /dna/layouts.json without retry or loading state for the archetype itself.
No focus trap or focus restoration for the modal dialog.
Members normalization suggests an API/schema mismatch between /api/channels and DNA's channelGraph adapter.
notes: This file is the host seam that frames DNA's channel fabric graph, handling open/close lifecycle and a small data-normalization shim.
questions: What is the exact shape of /api/channels members and why does DNA expect an array?
How is focus managed when the dialog opens and closes?
What happens if /dna/layouts.json is slow or returns no 'channel-view' archetype?
Is there a reason the graph is not rendered while loading is true even if cached channels exist?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it is documented as a full-width centered overlay that mounts DNA's live fabric graph, and the code does exactly that plus degrade handling and members normalization.
intention_signals: To expose channels as a visual, living network without writing a custom graph renderer, by delegating layout and rendering to a shared DNA archetype system.
novelty: The adapter that rewrites channel.members from object keys into an id array so an upstream DNA graph adapter can consume it, and the use of a generic renderArchetype with a hubNetwork SVG visual device.
contribution: Provides an overlay host and lifecycle for DNA's channel fabric graph, a data-normalization shim for channel members, and clean degradation when the DNA substrate or archetype is unavailable.
summary_for_embedding: ChannelView is a React overlay component that opens on a custom window event, renders loading/error/empty states, and mounts DNA's generic channel-view fabric graph after normalizing channel member shapes.
intention_for_embedding: To present every Company channel as a living, DNA-rendered network map inside a full-width centered modal overlay.

symbols.does: Describes the expected window.DNA interface used by this file.
symbols.description: A TypeScript type alias that declares the global DNA helpers this component relies on: faceRecord.channelGraph for graph record generation, org.hubNetwork for SVG network generation, renderArchetype for archetype rendering, and _channelViewArchetype for caching the fetched layout.

symbols.does: Returns the global window.DNA object cast to DNAGlobal.
symbols.description: A small helper function that accesses window.DNA and casts it to the local DNAGlobal type, centralizing the runtime dependency lookup used by the graph mounting effect.

symbols.does: Renders the fabric overlay and mounts DNA's channel graph.
symbols.description: The exported React component that manages overlay open/close state via channelStore, listens for the channels:open event and Escape key, renders loading/error/empty messages, and mounts the DNA fabric graph inside a full-width centered panel when channels are available.

symbols.does: Opens the channels overlay in response to the channels:open event.
symbols.description: A local event handler created inside ChannelView that calls openChannels() when the custom window event fires.

symbols.does: Closes the overlay when Escape is pressed while it is open.
symbols.description: A local keyboard event handler that checks whether the overlay is open and the Escape key was pressed, then calls closeChannels().
